key_insight: The paper reframes neural-network training and inference as Hamilton–Jacobi initial-value problems, with a single deformation parameter ε connecting deep learning, viscous PDEs, tropical algebra, and convex optimization.
engineering_relevance: This gives a unified PDE-based lens for analyzing model training, robustness, attribution, and scaling across architectures such as residual networks, transformers, and recurrent models.
method: Exact correspondence for log-sum-exp layers via the Hopf–Cole transform.
Generalizes structurally to residual networks, transformers, RNNs, LSTMs, and SSMs as discretizations of Hamilton–Jacobi equations.
Uses the co-state equation/Pontryagin framework to interpret backpropagation and derive influence/attribution behavior.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical results including an optimal generalization rate O(n^{-1/(d+2)}), an O(N) influence-function form, and scaling laws tied to intrinsic dimension, but does not mention empirical experiments.
watch_item: The framework relies on Lipschitz and architecture-specific assumptions, so practical use may be sensitive to numerical stability, discretization error, and whether the exact PDE correspondence holds beyond idealized settings.
paper_title: The Hamilton-Jacobi Theory of Deep Learning
